Please enter password. Please enter a valid email/phone number. Open navigation drawer Close navigation drawer LOGOUT HOME Application Version PROFILE PRIVACY POLICY OFFLINE SYNC Community Health Worker CHW Households Search Add Household Household No No of people Members Registered Filters %1$d/%2$d Investigation INVESTIGATION Prescription PRESCRIPTION COUNSELLING Counselling SUBMIT Household Name / No Diagnoses Confirm Diagnoses Patient Status Referred Edit Diagnoses Edit Status Patient Category General Edit Category Presenting Complaints Type your notes Clinical Notes Systemic Examinations Other None Assessment Household Members Finish Registration Add New Member Household Details House No Village Landmark Household Head Mobile Number %1$s - %2$s - %3$s Patient Medical Review

Household Name / No Member Deceased M F ICCM TB National ID Bio Data Name Gender %1$s Months %1$s Weeks %1$s Years Result Patient\'s Summary Could not be fetched Referred for Further Assessment Done General Danger Signs MUAC Cough Fever Breathing Rate Dispensed RDT Test Diarrhoea Signs Label Write your observation Notes Yes No Nearest PHU Referred to Was the child actually taken to the Clinic Next visit date Presumptive TB No TB Signs & Symptoms Symptoms No Symptoms Selected Symptoms Selected Select Symptoms OK Please check your internet connectivity. Incorrect email or password Close Error Alert Retry Session Expired! Please login again. You does not have internet to connect our system.Though you can login offline for offline features.Do you wish to continue? Role Email Phone No Assigned Health Facility SPICE Suite Access Back %1$s %2$s Registration Status Pending Finished Apply\ Filter (%1d) Villages Phone number should starts with %1$s or Edit Household Edit Member Select Member OKAY %1$s M %1$s W %1$s Y Household registration \n completed successfully. Household No : %1$s Patient ID Select Flow ANC PNC Childhood Visit %1$s - %2$s Location service disabled! Please turn on your Location service to get your current location to proceed Search Patient Advanced Search First Name Last Name Mobile Number %1d Patients No patients found to upload. Search by National ID Referral Ticket Cost Next Medical Review Summary Pregnancy Summary Pregnancy History Diagnosis Disease Category Disease Condition Prescriptions Medical Supplies Normal Moderate Malnutrition Severe Nutrition %1$s ( %2$s ) Severe Dehydration Pneumonia BPM Malaria %1$d ( %2$d days ) HH ID Date of Onset Date of Review Different Login! Last logged in user was %1$s. From this version it is prohibited Sync Status Something went wrong during upload. Please try again later %1$d record yet to sync Instructions Measuring MUAC Checking for Oedema Chest in-drawing Patient On Treatment No Referral / Treatment Required Select Ask the mother to remove any clothing that covers the child’s left arm. Identify the midpoint of the child’s left upper arm. This can be done by taking a piece of string (or the tape itself), place one end on the tip of the child’s shoulder and the other on the elbow Now bend the string up in a loop to double it so the point at the elbow is placed together with the point on the shoulder with a loop hanging down – the end of the straightened loop indicates the midpoint. Straighten the child’s arm and wrap the tape around the arm at the midpoint. Make sure the tape is flat around the skin. Inspect the tension of the tape on the child’s arm. Make sure the tape has the proper tension and is not too tight so that the skin is compressed or too loose so that the tape does not contact the skin all the way round the arm. When the tape is in the correct position on the arm with correct tension, pay attention to the color of MUAC Use your thumbs to press gently on the top of both feet at the same time and say “1001 1002 and 1003”. Lift the fingers and observe if a ‘pit’ remains The child has oedema if a pit remains in both feet when you lift your thumbs The child has chest in-drawing if the lower chest wall goes IN when the child breathes IN. Chest in-drawing is not visible when the child breathes out. For chest in-drawing to be present, it must be clearly visible and present at every breath. "Last Menstrual Period " Gestational Age Estimated Delivery Date Refer Patient Treatment Next visit date reload Select Review Date Selected Referral Date Referral Reason Referred To Referred By Date Of Delivery Date Type Labour/Delivery Date of Delivery Date of Labour onset Delivery Type Delivery At No of Neonates Time of Delivery Time of Labour Onset Delivery By Delivery Status Please enter valid input Select Date Number Hour Minutes Member registration \n completed successfully. Patient ID : %1$s
Mother Signs/Symptoms observed General Condition Of Mother Risk Factors State Of The Perineum Episotomy Tear TT Dose taken so far Status Neonate Neonate Outcome Male Female State Of Baby Abnormal Birth Weight (in Kg) Signs/Symptoms observed State of the Baby APGAR Score 1 Deg 2 Deg 3 Deg 4 Deg Labour/Delivery Medical Review Labour/Delivery
